_metadata.name: Restored SPIKE-11 PRIMARY-measure values (v1 scale)
_metadata.supersedes: v2 Option-A Phase-A stop-gap (PR #66). v2 anchored its absolute scale to a single un-normalized, quiet native read. Issue #67/#70 root-caused that deficit as a MISSING wav2vec2 input normalization (do_normalize), not a calibration error — fixed in the input-normalization PR. With normalized input the SPIKE-11 v1 scale is reachable at any capture level, so the band-aid is obsolete.
_metadata.values_note: table values are byte-identical to v1 (PR #32, SPIKE-11). version bumped 2 -> 3 (not reset to 1) so the persisted threshold_table_version unambiguously identifies which physical table scored a session: 1 = original v1, 2 = Option-A band-aid, 3 = restored v1 scale.
_metadata.calibration: gentle = p10, standard = p25, strict = p50 of the SPIKE-11 correct-production allophone-aware certainty distribution (PRIMARY measure).
_metadata.depends_on: the issue #67 input-normalization fix MUST be present in the running binary. Without it, quiet captures re-introduce the over-flagging these thresholds assume normalized input avoids.
_metadata.open_followup_precondition: #69 — theta has n=1 target position in visiting_nyc.ipa.json (zero statistical weight). Resolve before ANY threshold re-derivation; it gates whether a re-anchor is even meaningful for theta.
_metadata.open_followup_resweep: optional clean re-anchor (after #69): re-run spike/eval/l2arctic_correlation.py WITH the same normalization added to run_inference, then re-derive.
